title: Archer Aviation partners with defense company Anduril for DoD contract
text: Anduril and Archer announced an exclusive partnership to jointly develop a hybrid VTOL aircraft for critical defense applications targeting a potential program of record from the Department of Defense. With Archer's ability to rapidly develop advanced VTOL aircraft using existing commercial parts and supply chains combined with Anduril's deep expertise in artificial intelligence, missionization, and systems integration, the partnership will accelerate speed to market for critical hybrid VTOL capabilities at a fraction of the cost of more traditional alternatives. To help fund Archer Defense and for other general corporate purposes, Archer raised $430M in additional equity capital, with participation from longtime strategic partners Stellantis and United Airlines as well as new institutional investors, including Wellington Management and Abu Dhabi investment holding company 2PointZero. A $142M Agility Prime contract and $2B liquidity position the company for R&D scaling and defense contract execution. Archer announced two strategic acquisitions to accelerate the development of its next-generation defense aircraft including one of a patent portfolio and hiring of key talent from Overair, and the other of key composite manufacturing assets and a ~60,000 square foot manufacturing facility from Mission Critical Composites. Anduril UK, Archer, and GKN Aerospace announced a teaming agreement in support of work with the British Army and Ministry of Defence.
